OPPOS TO OSC AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AND ATTORNEYS FEES FILED BY DEFENDANT ROSS, WALTER L.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY DEFENDANT WALTER ROSS SHOULD NOT BE EXCLUDED FROM 60 GATES STREET. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE IS DENIED. COMMISSIONER GARGANO'S ORDER DOES NOT PERMIT PLAINTIFF TO EXCLUDE DEFENDANT INDEFINITELY FROM THE PREMISES, AND DOES NOT GIVE PLAINTIFF FULL POSSESSION OF THE PREMISES. PLAINTIFF MAY SEEK A COURT ORDER SETTING FORTH SPECIFIC DATES AND TIMES WHEN PLAINTIFF NEEDS TO BE PRESENT AND/OR BRING THIRD PARTIES ONTO THE PREMISES FOR REPAIRS, PREPARATIONS, SHOWING, ETC. DEFENDANT'S ORAL REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. COUNSEL ARE TO MEET AND CONFER PRIOR TO ANY FURTHER MOTIONS REGARDING THE SHOWING OF THE HOUSE OR COMMISSIONER GARGANO'S ORDER. THERE WILL BE COOPERATION. THE COURT WILL NEED A DECLARATION FROM A PERSON, IF THERE IS A PROBLEM. (DEFENSE COUNSEL WILL PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE COURT'S SIGNATURE.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Walter Ross Should Not Be Excluded From 60 Gates Street
Off Calendar May-26-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff's Order to Show cause why Defendant Walter Ross Should Not be Excluded from 60 Gates Street. Appearances: Leroy W. Rice, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 673-7800 appearing for Petitioner and Moving party; Randal Frank Blair, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 205-3766 appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Order to Show Cause is denied. Commissioner Gargano's order does not permit plaintiff to exclude defendant indefinitely from the premises, and does not give plaintiff full possession of the premises. Plaintiff may seek a court order setting forth specific dates and times when plaintiff needs to be present and/or bring third parties onto the premises for repairs, preparations, showing, etc. Defendant's oral request for sanctions is denied without prejudice. Counsel are to meet and confer prior to any further motions regarding the showing of the house or Commissioner Gargano's Order. There will be cooperation. The Court will need a Declaration from a person, if there is a problem. (Defense counsel will prepare proposed Order for the Court's signature.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Walter Ross Should Not Be Excluded From 60 Gates Street

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Apr-06-2010 Continued To Sep-16-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

LeRoy W. Rice, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Richard T. Ross, who was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Walter L. Ross.
Default was entered against defendant on June 10, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, April 6, 2010.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Richard T. Ross, and against the Defaulted Defendant Walter L. Ross as follows:

Defendant Walter L. Ross has no interest in the subject premises, located at 60 Gates Street, San Francisco, CA, and that plaintiff Richard T. Ross is the sole owner of said property. Further Orders of the Court are set forth on pages 4 & 5 of the interlocutory judgment.

Matter is continued to September 16, 2010 @ 9:00 a.m. in Department re: Final Judgment.

Interlocutory Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Damages/Principal $22,611.87
Prejudgment Interest $2,068.13
Attorney's Fees $
Costs $320.00, for a total of $
Judgment signed in open court / Counsel to prepare Judgment.

Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

LeRoy W. Rice, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Richard T. Ross, who was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Walter L. Ross.
Default was entered against defendant on June 10, 2010.
Doe defendants have not been dismissed.

Counsel for plaintiff was unable to provide the Court with proof that a Lis Pendens was recorded at the San Francisco County Clerk's Office.

Good cause appearing, this matter is continued to April 6, 2010 @ 9:00 a.m. in Department 218.
